answers.where: Old Gate town forge, with the forge/workbench and mine route visible in the main scene.
answers.action: Use the cache shards to forge or equip the Mine Lantern, then open the next mine depth route.
answers.response: The lantern is shown as a crafted result in the scene and in the equipment/result rail.
answers.reward: The player gets a visible Mine Lantern upgrade and a Depth 2 route promise.
answers.game_look: The screen now reads as a fantasy browser RPG equipment event: location art, hero, blacksmith, route strip, result icons, and persistent ornate chrome all support the action.

issues.text: Direction target contains a few small decorative pseudo-glyph marks; source-family generation must keep runtime text areas blank and isolated.
This is still a fused fake shot, not reusable runtime UI; source families and slice9/crop manifests are required before integration.

problem: Direction target is usable for the next art pass but is not native runtime proof or final reusable UI.
next: Generate or derive separate town forge background, lantern/resource icons, character/forge FX, and frame/source families; then integrate the smallest native forge visual pass and capture state_town_lantern_upgrade_v2.png.
